lines.text: Central Perk, Joey, Monica, and Chandler are sitting on the couch and chair as Phoebe is getting coffee.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (coming from the bathroom) Hey Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm having dinner with my dad tomorrow night, do you wanna come?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure. Yeah, he's kinda sexy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no, no, I'll be there too.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay so, we'll just come up with some kind of signal if it's going well you can take off.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No Phoebe! I just need you there for support. I haven't told him I'm pregnant yet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. Why not?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: 'Cause I know he's gonna flip out and I hate it when he's angry.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh Rachel, this is all so 'Papa don't preach.'

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What Phoebe? Wait! One time he caught me smoking he said if he ever saw me doing that again he'd make me eat the entire pack.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! Oh well, I will be there!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Gosh. I'm not gonna let that man make you eat your baby. (They both sit down by the rest of the gang and Phoebe recognizes a man by the window.) Oh. Hey! Who is that guy? I think I know him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (recognizing him and panicking) No you don't!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (panicking) No you don't.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!!! Monica!! He's the stripper from your bachelorette party!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Her what?!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Your secret bachelorette party...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You had a bachelorette party?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She untied his G-string with her teeth. (Pause) Somebody stop me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I thought we weren't gonna have bachelor/bachelorette parties! Y'know, we agreed that it was a silly tradition.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's a grand tradition!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry, they surprised me. There was nothing I could do!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well you could've untied it with your hands.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This is so unfair! The one thing I wanted to do was throw my best friend a bachelor party, but no, I wasn't allowed to. All I got was a stupid steak dinner!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You went home with the waitress.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah, that was a pretty good night.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't believe you didn't tell me! You know that the two pillars of marriage are openness and honesty!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ugh, I knew giving you that book was gonna come back and bite me in the ass!
Opening Credits
A Restaurant, Rachel and Phoebe are having dinner with Dr. Green, and everyone is looking at the menu.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: How about I order everyone the Moroccan chicken?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I-I don't eat meat.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: It's chicken.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I don't eat that either.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: I'll never understand you lesbians. (To Rachel) So baby, tell me...what is new with you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well actually umm...

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: (interrupting) Your '74 Lafite sir.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: '74?! I ordered the '75! That's a magnificent wine! The '74 is sewage! Why would you bring me sewage?! (The waiter's dumbfounded) Is that a hard question? Are you an idiot? Is that why you're a waiter?

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: This is why I told the manager I wouldn't wait on you tonight! (Runs off.)

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Oh come on! Don't be such a baby! (Goes after him)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (To Phoebe) In case you didn't notice, that is a scary man.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He's right though, the '74 is absolute piss.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: This was such a huge mistake. I can't tell him Phoebe. I can't, I can't, I can't, I can't...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Rachel!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No it's okay, this is what's gonna happen. I'm gonna wait a couple years and then the baby will tell him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why, so he can get mad at the baby?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, that is the...baby's problem. (Dr. Green returns.) Oh, everything okay with the waiter?

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: I have no idea, I went to the bathroom. So sweetie, you were starting to tell me what is uh, what is new with you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well... Umm, I got TiVo.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: What's TiVo?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's slang for pregnant.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Are you really pregnant?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well uh, yes and no. Except not no. So to sum it up, yeah.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Who is the father? Oh no! Please don't tell me it's her! (Points at Phoebe.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, it's Ross. It's Ross. You like Ross. (He just shakes his head.) Oh daddy, I hope you're okay with all of this. I mean think about it, this is a good thing. You're gonna-This is your first grandchild! You're gonna be a poppy!

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: That's true.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: (laughs) Poppy. (To Phoebe) Oh, I'm gonna be a poppy. (Stops laughing) So when is the wedding?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: The wedding! There's going to be a wedding. Young lady, don't you sit there and tell me my first grandchild is going to be a bastard! (Rachel pauses) Rachel Karen Green, tell me there is gonna be a wedding!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: February 2nd!
Dr. Green exhales in relief.
Central Perk, Chandler and Joey are reading a magazine and Ross is chatting with Mona.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: So it was really cool seeing you lecture today.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh thanks. Although it kinda seemed like you were falling asleep there a little.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Oh no-no, I-I had my eyes closed so I could concentrate and y'know take it all in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, a lot of my students do that.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: So, I gotta get going.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I-I'll see you tonight.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, bye.
They kiss and she starts to leave.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Oh hey, thanks again for showing me your semi-precious stone collection. It was amazing! (She leaves.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: My God! You must be good in bed!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So uh, you and Mona, been a while now. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, it's good. It's going good. I mean, we get along great. She's, she's so...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hot?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I was gonna say sweet, but yeah-huh!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She's okay with Rachel and the baby?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well I...I haven't actually told her yet. I don't want to scare her off, y'know?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you have to honest with her! Otherwise you may think that you're going down the same path, but you're really going down different ones.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm gonna take that book and beat you to death with it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Oh my God! You are gonna love me so much! I felt really bad about the whole bachelorette party thing, so tonight you're gonna have a bachelor party.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I got this number from this guy at work and I hired a stripper to come dance for you. Am I going in the wife hall of fame or what?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Honey! That's crazy! I don't want you to get me a stripper...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Will you let the lady talk?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on! Come on, it'll be fun! It'll make me feel so much better.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I appreciate it, but uh, it's a little creepy. Y'know? I'm not a bachelor anymore.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So don't think of it as a bachelor party, think of it as a...a two month anniversary present.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure, one year is paper, but two months is lapdance! (Joey nods his agreement.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Please! I feel so bad! Just watch the hot woman get naked!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right fine! But I'm only doing this for you!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And Joey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you. All right, now who else do you want to invite?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ah, no-no-no just Ross. Ross and Joey is embarrassing enough.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh actually, sorry I can't even make it. I'm seeing Mona again tonight.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I understand: who would cancel an actual date to go to a fake bachelor party?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (on cell phone) I'm sorry I gotta cancel tonight baby...
The Restaurant, dinner has ended and Phoebe and Rachel are talking. Dr. Green is not at the table.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry I won't be able to make it to your imaginary wedding, but I'm really busy that day. Yeah, I already have a unicorn baptism and a leprechaun bar mitzvah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know. I know. I panicked, I panicked. I didn't want him to start yelling at me like I was some '74 Latour.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's Lafite. The '74 Latour is actually drinking quite nicely.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right here he comes. I'm gonna do this, I'm gonna tell him, I'm gonna be strong.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: I just called a friend of mine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: I think I may be able to book The Plaza on short notice.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?! The Plaza?!! Oh daddy!! (Hugs him and Phoebe glares at her.) Right. Daddy, I need to talk to you. Please, sit down.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: What is it sweetie?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: There's not gonna be a wedding. Ross and I are not getting married.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry daddy.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: I don't believe this!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh now daddy, stay calm. Please.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Stay calm?!! How do you expect me to stay calm?! This is unacceptable Rachel! And I wanna know why?!! Is it because that punk Ross won't marry you?! That's it! Is that it?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes. Yes, he says I'm damaged goods.

Monica and Chandler's, Chandler's bachelor party has begun, what there is of it, with only Joey (wearing a gold paper top hat) and Chandler (wearing what appears to be a Burger King paper crown) enjoying a nice product placement of Budweisers on ice.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So you uh, nervous about getting married?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, look let's pretend it's a real bachelor party. Okay? Y'know? Before your wedding. Come on, it'll be fun.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. I can't believe tomorrow's the big day.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How does it feel knowing you're never gonna be with another woman again huh? Knowing you're gonna have to wake up to the same face everyday until you finally have the sweet release of death.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're right, this is more fun.
There's a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's her! Okay, come on! (They go over and open the door.)

lines.speaker: Stripper
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi. (Joey blows on a noisemaker.)

lines.speaker: Stripper
lines.text: So which one of you lucky boys is Chandler?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, that-that's-that's me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Joey Tribbiani, a big fan.

lines.speaker: Stripper
lines.text: So is that a bedroom? (Points to the guestroom.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, yeah right over there.

lines.speaker: Stripper
lines.text: All right, whenever you're ready. (She goes into the bedroom.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That was weird.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why-why would she go in the bedroom?

lines.speaker: Stripper
lines.text: I'm waiting.
They both slowly enter the bedroom and quickly walk back out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So she's a...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, that's one naked hooker!
Ross's Apartment, Ross and Mona are sitting on the couch.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: I love your place! Where is this guy from? (A statue from the top of his apothecary table.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh that's an eighteenth century Indian artifact from Calcutta.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Oh wow! So, you're more than just dinosaurs.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So much more.
They start making out and she kicks the eighteenth century Indian artifact from Calcutta off of his apothecary table from the days of yore and the magical city of White Plains.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Oh my God! Oh my God! I'm so sorry!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Aw forget it, it's from Pier One. (There's an angry knock on the door.) Sorry. (Goes and opens the door to an irate Dr. Green.)

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: You think you can knock up my daughter and then not marry her?! I'm gonna kill you!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know this is actually not a great time for me.
Commercial Break
Ross's Apartment, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: So? Come on! Explain yourself Geller! First you get my Rachel pregnant!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: You got Rachel pregnant?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Who did?!

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: You did!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes. Yes, yes I did. (To Mona) But-but it was, it was just a one night thing. It meant nothing.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Oh? Really? That's what my daughter means to you? Nothing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No sir umm, she means a lot to me. I mean, I care-I-I love Rachel.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Mona) Oh but not that way. I mean...I mean I'm not in love with her. I love her like a, like a friend.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Oh really? That's how treat a friend? You get her in trouble and then refuse to marry her?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Dr. Green) Hey! I offered to marry her!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Wh...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Mona) But I didn't want to.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: Well why not? So you can spend your time with this tramp?!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Tramp?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sorry. Dr. Green, Mona. Mona, Dr. Green.
Monica and Chandler's, Chandler and Joey are discussing what to do about the now naked hooker in the guestroom.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't believe there is a naked hooker in there!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wait! Wait! Maybe she's a hooker and a stripper, but she got confused about what she's supposed to do.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Could be. I mean technically she did strip, we just, we just missed it. (Walks towards the bedroom.) Ma'am, are you also a stripper?

lines.speaker: Hooker
lines.text: Uh, no. But I could pretend to strip, but that's gonna cost extra. Okay, here's the extras, handcuffs, spanking... (Chandler grunts for her not to continue and Joey pulls him back into the kitchen.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Maybe Monica's playing a joke on ya. Y'know? Getting her own husband a hooker, that's pretty funny.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That is funny, maybe for my birthday she'll murder someone.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I bet Ross was in on it too. I mean he was conveniently busy.

lines.speaker: Hooker
lines.text: Do you mind if I smoke in here?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh actually, I'd rather you...Yeah, go ahead. We're gonna have to burn that room down anyway.
Ross's Apartment, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: How could you have kept all of this from me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I was going to tell you, but...

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: But what?! You figured you'd get what you wanted and then dump her like you dumped Rachel!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! I did not dump Rachel! (To Mona) Nor are we still together. (The phone rings and Ross goes to answer it, only he's trapped behind the apothecary table by Dr. Green.) Can I just... (Dr. Green glares at him.) Why don't we just let the machine get that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (on machine) Hey Ross. It's Joey. There's a hooker over here and we thought maybe you'd know something about it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No! No! No! No! I-I-I-I-I need to, I need to lie down.
Monica's Restaurant's Kitchen, she's cooking as one of her waiters, Stu, comes over to talk to her.

lines.speaker: Stu
lines.text: So, tonight's the night of the big bachelor party?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! Hey! Thanks for getting me that girl's number.

lines.speaker: Stu
lines.text: No problem. So who's the party for?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: My husband.

lines.speaker: Stu
lines.text: You hired your husband a hooker?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She's a stripper.

lines.speaker: Stu
lines.text: No, she's a hooker.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is that, is that what they call strippers sometimes?

lines.speaker: Stu
lines.text: When they're hookers.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God Stu! I-I can't believe you did this! Now are you absolutely sure she's a hooker?

lines.speaker: Stu
lines.text: Either that or she's just the best, most expensive date I ever had.
Monica runs out.
Monica and Chandler's, Joey and Chandler are still deciding what to do about the hooker.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Maybe she meant to get you a hooker.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why would she do that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Maybe she wants you to learn something. Huh? Now is there anything you're really bad at y'know, sexually?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This is the worst bachelor party ever!

lines.speaker: Hooker
lines.text: What's taking you boys so long?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: In a minute!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Joey) In a minute? What's gonna happen in a minute?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, all right maybe-maybe you should just ask her to leave.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why me?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! It's your bachelor party.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Which is why you should do it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't want to. You do it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You do it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You do it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right Rock, Paper, Scissors who has to tell the whore to leave! (Joey smirks.) What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I miss this.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't think we've actually done this before!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I-I miss hanging out with you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well we...we still hang out.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Not like we used to. Remember? You and me used to be inseparable. Y'know now it's like...things are different.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well y'know, things are different. I'm...I'm married now.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh sure-And hey, don't get me wrong, I am so happy for you guys. I just...I miss...hanging out...just-just us, y'know?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I miss that too. I tell you what; from now on we'll make time to hang out with each other.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You got it. Come here. (They hug and are observed by the hooker.)

lines.speaker: Hooker
lines.text: Oh God! Listen, I am this close to robbing you guys. (Does the close sign.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering quickly) She's a hooker! She's a hooker! She's a... (Stops as she sees her.) Hi! Uh, we spoke on the phone. (Goes and shakes the hooker's hand.)
Joey and Rachel's, Rachel and Phoebe are at the counter as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So your dad dropped by. He's a pleasant man!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (quietly) Oh no...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'd better go. (She goes and sits down in the La-Z-Boy E-cliner 3000, the chair that Sit magazine called chair of the year, and they both look at her.) Just over here: I don't want to miss the fight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross I'm so sorry. Okay. I-I will promise I will straighten this out with him tomorrow in person, or via e-mail.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't care about your dad! I care about Mona! She was there and now she's totally freaked out!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh okay, I'll fix that to. What's her e-mail address?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rachel!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, I promise. I'll fix this. I swear. I'll-I'll-I'll-I'll talk to her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's it?! You call that a fight? Come on! "We were on a break!" "No we weren't!" What happened to you two?!
Ross's Apartment, Ross is opening the door to Mona. Rachel is there as well.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you so much for coming back over.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Oh good, you're here. Yeah, and I was worried that it was going to be uncomfortable.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know Mona, just hear me out. First of all, I'm so sorry about my father yelling at you, but I heard you totally held your own. You're gonna have to tell me how you did that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Focus.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Um...But-Okay, yes Ross and I used to date. And yes we are gonna have a baby. But we are definitely not getting back together.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: How can I be sure on that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh we just-we drove each other crazy!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I mean he was possessive, he was jealous, he could never just let the little things go!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Trying to date this woman.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Right! But, none of that compared to how kind and-and how gentle and thoughtful he is. (Rubbing his shoulder.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Probably shouldn't touch me.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Y'know, I-I-I just...I don't want to get in the middle of something so complicated.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, I get it, but Mona, what relationship is not complicated? I mean we all have our baggage! You must too! Why else would you still be single? (Mona looks at her.) I am so gonna leave right now. (Ross opens the door for her and she leaves.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Should I leave this open for you too?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: I'm not sure yet. Why didn't you just tell me about all this?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Because what's going on with Rachel has nothing to do with how I feel about you.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Yeah? Well you still shoulda told me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know and I was going to, but I thought it was better that you heard it from Rachel's father. Look I...I made a mistake, but it's only because I really, really like you. Really!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Okay, I guess you can...close the door now. (He does so and they kiss.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Forgot my purse! (Sees them kissing.) Oh, you guys made up. (To Mona) He's a good kisser isn't he? (Ross goes to close the door on her.) I'm going! (Quickly leaves and Ross locks the door.)
Monica and Chandler's, the bachelor party has ended and Monica is trying to explain herself to Chandler.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I swear I didn't know she was a hooker! I mean wh-Did you let her smoke in here?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Her ass print is still on your grandmother's quilt, do you really want to talk about smoking?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know what? I'm gonna make this up to you. I promised you a stripper (turns on the radio), and you're gonna get a stripper. (She starts to strip.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica! Wait!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (puts on his crown) Carry on.
She does so by taking off her jacket seductively, only she has trouble getting one hand out and slams the jacket on the chair angrily to remove it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ooh, these tennis shoes are so tight. I think I'll take them off. (Goes to do so.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Could you not narrate?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Gotcha sailor. (Kicks one of her shoes off and it lands in the kitchen knocking something down, but she continues to strip.)
Closing Credits
Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is being yelled at by her dad over the phone, and he's been going on for so long Rachel is holding the phone away from her ear and reading a book.

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: (on phone) ...just because you're not in love with the guy you can't...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering, quietly) Wow, you told your dad the truth.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: About an hour ago.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wanna go see a movie?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! (She gets up and sets the phone on the counter without hanging it up.) Bye daddy. (Phoebe and her leave.)

lines.speaker: Dr. Green
lines.text: (on phone, not hearing her) ...there's gonna be a wedding! (Joey enters from his room and goes to get a beer from the fridge.) That's unacceptable Rachel! What the hell does love have to do with it anyway?! There are more important things in a marriage other than love! (Joey hears something and looks around for the source.) ...constantly thinking about things! You have to think about the consequences of your decision. (Joey finds that the sound is coming from the phone and puts it to his ear.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (on phone) Hey! I do too think about the consequences of my decisions! (Listens) What gives you the right to... (Listens) Go to hell! (Hangs up the phone and opens the fridge.) Stupid guy on my phone.
End
